name: Directus
description: Open data platform that turns any SQL database into a headless CMS with REST and GraphQL APIs

description.value: Open data platform that turns any SQL database into a headless CMS with REST and GraphQL APIs

license.comment: Business Source License 1.1; free for organizations with <$5M annual revenue/funding

initial-release.comment: Originally released as Flash-based database admin GUI; later versions written in PHP, current version is TypeScript

deployment-model.comment: Self-hosted option available; Directus Cloud is the managed offering

database-support.comment: Also supports MariaDB, MS-SQL, OracleDB, CockroachDB

api-type.comment: Auto-generated REST and GraphQL APIs from database schema; WebSocket support for realtime

multi-tenancy.comment: Supports multiple projects through separate databases or role-based separation

static-site-generation.comment: Works with any SSG via REST/GraphQL APIs; official examples for Gatsby, Astro, Next.js, etc.

edge-deployment.comment: Directus itself runs as a Node.js server; can serve content via CDN but the app isn't edge-deployed

content-types.comment: Create unlimited custom collections (content types) via UI or code

custom-fields.comment: 35+ field types with extensive configuration options

field-types.comment: Full suite: text, WYSIWYG, file/image, relational, JSON, repeater blocks, translations, geometry, color picker, and more

content-relationships.comment: Full relational support: O2O, O2M, M2O, M2M, and M2A (many-to-any)

nested-components.comment: M2A relationships enable page builder functionality with reusable blocks; block editor interface available

localization.comment: Built-in translations interface with specialized relational field; auto-creates language collections

content-validation.comment: Field-level validation rules configurable per field

embedded-interactives.comment: Visual Editor beta and block editor allow embedding interactive components; M2A enables flexible widget placement

content-versioning.comment: Built-in content versioning with side-by-side comparison modal for reviewing changes

draft-publish.comment: Configurable status field with draft/published states; supports custom workflow stages

scheduled-publishing.comment: Achievable via Flows automation; schedule CRON triggers to publish content at specified times

approval-workflows.comment: Custom approval workflows via status fields and role-based permissions; email notifications available

content-locking.comment: Lockable items via status field and permissions system

audit-logging.comment: Activity Log tracks all data-changing actions: create, update, delete, comment, login with user and timestamp

media-library.comment: Built-in Files module for asset management

image-transformations.comment: Full Sharp library integration for resize, crop, format conversion; supports focal points

external-media-storage.comment: Supports S3, Azure, Cloudinary, Google Cloud Storage; multiple storage adapters can run simultaneously

video-support.comment: Native video file upload; streaming requires external service integration

svg-support.comment: SVG files supported via Sharp library integration

role-based-access.comment: Granular role-based access control; users see only permitted data

granular-permissions.comment: Field-level and collection-level permissions; conditional access based on item values

sso-saml.comment: Supports OAuth 2.0, OpenID, LDAP, and SAML; integrations with Google, Microsoft, Okta

team-collaboration.comment: Comments on items, activity notifications, multi-user editing

audience-restrictions.comment: Role and permission-based content restrictions for different user groups

typescript-sdk.comment: Official @directus/sdk package with full TypeScript support; community SDKs for Python, Go, Dart, PHP

content-type-sdk.comment: Data models can be defined via code and synced with directus-sync CLI

cli-tools.comment: npx directus for server management; create-directus-extension for extensions; directus-template-cli for project scaffolding

local-development.comment: Full local development via npm/Docker; npx directus init for quick setup

webhooks.comment: Built-in Flows automation with webhook triggers and HTTP request operations

plugin-system.comment: Extensions SDK for custom modules, interfaces, layouts, endpoints, hooks, and operations

admin-ui-customization.comment: Fully white-label capable; custom modules, interfaces, layouts, and themes via Extensions SDK

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive docs with tutorials, API reference, video content (Directus TV); active community

frontend-frameworks.comment: Official examples for all major frameworks; ~40% of developers use Next.js or Nuxt

search-integration.comment: Via extensions: searchsync for Elasticsearch/Algolia/MeiliSearch; official tutorials for custom hook integrations

preview-mode.comment: Built-in Live Preview with split-screen in Data Studio; works with content versioning for draft preview

ecommerce-integration.comment: Cloud templates for eCommerce; integrations via Flows automation and webhooks

analytics-integration.comment: Built-in Insights module for data visualization; external analytics via webhooks/Flows

ai-features.comment: 10 AI operation extensions: AI Writer (GPT/Claude/Llama), Image Generation (DALL-E), Translation (DeepL), Transcription (Deepgram), Text Intelligence

cdn-included.comment: Directus Cloud includes CDN; self-hosted requires separate CDN setup

response-caching.comment: Built-in caching layer; configurable cache adapters

rate-limits.comment: Professional: 250K API requests/month; Enterprise: customizable; self-hosted unlimited

global-regions.comment: Professional: 3 regions; Enterprise: 20+ global deployment regions

free-tier.comment: Self-hosted is free for orgs with <$5M annual revenue; no free Cloud tier

open-source.comment: Source available under BSL-1.1; converts to open source after 4 years; free for most users

pricing-model.comment: Cloud Professional: $99/mo + $15/user; Enterprise: custom; self-hosted free under $5M revenue

commercial-support.comment: Enterprise tier includes 24/7 critical issue response and premium support

enterprise-features.comment: SAML/LDAP SSO, dedicated infrastructure, multiple environments, SLA, direct database access
